name: Event Timing Diagnostic
note:
Proves event ordering and dedup behaviour under the reactive listener-per-rule architecture.

WHAT TO WATCH
  Each rule fires a labelled toast. Count how many times each toast appears.
  D4 also appends a visible marker to the message — count those too.

EXPECTED (if everything is correct)
  D1: 1 toast — at the very start of generation, before any tokens
  D2: 1 toast — after message committed (toast-only rule, no side effects)
  D3: 1 toast — after message committed, sets diag-gate
  D4: 1 toast + 1 appended marker — fires on MESSAGE_RECEIVED when diag-gate not yet set
       (D3 and D4 both react to MESSAGE_RECEIVED concurrently; D4 fires because
        D3 hasn't set the gate yet at the moment D4 evaluates — this is intentional)
  D5: 1 toast — reactive wake on var:diag-gate being written by D3

RED FLAGS
  D2 fires 2 toasts  → toast-causes-stream-registration: the rule is registered at
                       both stream and postMessage stages because toast has
                       stage:[stream,postMessage]. Both evaluators fire on MESSAGE_RECEIVED.
  D4 fires 2 toasts  → same issue, or dedup broken for when:all dual-trigger rules
  D4 marker appears twice in the message → update(append) ran twice — dedup definitely broken
  D5 fires 2 toasts  → var:diag-gate notification woke both stream and postMessage evaluators
  D1 never fires     → GENERATION_STARTED flag not set correctly
  D4 never fires     → dual-trigger (when:all) is always blocked — check that MESSAGE_RECEIVED
                       fires before D3 sets diag-gate (it should, they're concurrent)

CONNECTION TO INNER THOUGHTS
  The inner-thoughts fallback rule is structured exactly like D4: when:all, event +
  var-not-set. It also contains toast. If D2 fires twice, it-fallback fires its toast
  twice. If D4's marker appears twice, the update(append) in it-fallback runs twice —
  which would explain doubled inner thoughts in the message.

rules.name: D1: GENERATION_STARTED
rules.note: Fires once at generation start, before any stream tokens. Should toast before D2-D5.

rules.name: D2: MESSAGE_RECEIVED (event + toast only)
rules.note: Single event trigger, single toast action. Because toast has stage:[stream,postMessage], this rule is registered at both stages. Both evaluators subscribe to flag:MESSAGE_RECEIVED and both fire when the flag is set — giving 2 toasts if the stream-evaluator issue is present.

rules.name: D3: Gate setter (MESSAGE_RECEIVED → writes diag-gate)
rules.note: Writes diag-gate on MESSAGE_RECEIVED. Because D3 and D4 both subscribe to flag:MESSAGE_RECEIVED, they start concurrently — D4 evaluates before D3 has written the gate. This is the same race the inner-thoughts rules face.

rules.name: D4: Fallback mirror (MESSAGE_RECEIVED + diag-gate not-set)
rules.note: Exact structural mirror of the inner-thoughts fallback rule: when:all, event trigger + var-match not-set, with compose + update(append) + toast. If this fires twice, the doubling mechanism is confirmed. Count both the toast and the appended marker in the message.

actions.value:
---
**[DIAG D4 FIRED]** — count this marker; 2 copies = update(append) ran twice

actions.title: D4 FIRED
actions.message: D4 fallback mirror — expect 1 toast + 1 message marker per turn

rules.name: D5: Var reactor (diag-gate is set)
rules.note: Subscribes to var:diag-gate. Wakes when D3 sets the gate. Proves reactive var-watching. Two toasts here = stream+postMessage evaluators both firing on the same var notification.

actions.title: D5 REACT
actions.message: D5: diag-gate is set — expect 1 toast, 2 = stream-evaluator bug
